rationale: Standard II(A) prohibits acting on material nonpublic information, but the mosaic theory permits conclusions drawn from public information combined with individually non-material nonpublic details. In vignette_a the former employee handed Priya the exact unreleased earnings figure — material nonpublic information — so trading on it violates II(A); in vignette_b the same call yields only a non-material observation about quiet dock activity, so her conclusion is a permissible mosaic. The distractors (trading for clients, doing a week of public research, or the source being a former employee) are identical or near-identical across both cases and do not change the outcome.

spans.rationale: A precise, still-unreleased earnings number is material and nonpublic, so it is the MNPI II(A) bars using.

spans.phrase: sells the company out of her clients' portfolios
spans.rationale: Trading on that MNPI is the prohibited act under II(A).
